task_completion.description: Page size is set to A6 and margins are set to 1.0 cm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a page setup or format dialog showing A6 size (105x148mm or 10.5x14.8cm) and 1.0cm (10mm) margins, or observe the on-screen rulers reflecting a total page width of approximately 10.5cm.

task_completion.description: Body text font size is changed to 9pt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the font size dropdown in the toolbar showing '9' while the document's body text is selected or being edited.

task_completion.description: The five main section titles are formatted with the 'Heading 1' style.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the section headings (e.g., 'Incident Command Structure', 'Medical Triage') appearing larger and bolder than the body text, or the style dropdown in the toolbar showing 'Heading 1' when a heading is selected.

task_completion.description: An automatic Table of Contents is inserted after the introduction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a structured Table of Contents block (often with a grey background or dotted leaders connecting to page numbers) located after the introductory text.

task_completion.description: The radio frequencies are converted into a 3-column table with headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a visible table structure (grid lines) with exactly 3 columns in the 'Radio Communications' section, containing the radio frequencies (e.g., 155.750, 155.235) and appropriate column headers.

task_completion.description: The document is saved after all formatting is complete.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking the 'Save' icon, using the File > Save menu, or the filename in the title bar lacking an unsaved changes indicator (such as an asterisk) at the end of the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical word processor to edit the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent opens and edits the .odt file in a GUI word processor (like Calligra Words or LibreOffice Writer), rather than using a plain text editor or terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's table feature rather than manually spacing text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent uses an 'Insert Table' or 'Convert Text to Table' dialog/tool, and that actual table grid lines are visible, rather than manually aligning text with spaces or tabs.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's automatic Table of Contents feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent uses an 'Insert Table of Contents' dialog or menu option, rather than manually typing out the headings and page numbers.

The five headings to be formatted as 'Heading 1' are: 'Incident Command Structure', 'Search and Rescue Operations', 'Medical Triage', 'Radio Communications', and 'Hazard Assessment'.
